title: podman_container does not recreate the container if root image is changed from read-only to read-write
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Changed read_only from true to commented out (so default: false) but the container was not recreated.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create container read-only

2. Comment out read-only: true

3. Attempt to recreate

**Describe the results you received:**

Read-only container remained.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Read-only container replaced with one whose root is read-write.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Found that read_only is not a setting tested for change so implemented this patch which appears to do the right thing. Changed params['read_only'] to default to False as per docs so that the comparison works if commented out.

```
--- plugins/module_utils/podman/podman_container_lib.py.old     2022-03-02 15:22:27.688204879 +1100
+++ plugins/module_utils/podman/podman_container_lib.py 2022-03-03 18:04:50.224838188 +1100
@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@
     publish=dict(type='list', elements='str', aliases=[
         'ports', 'published', 'published_ports']),
     publish_all=dict(type='bool'),
-    read_only=dict(type='bool'),
+    read_only=dict(type='bool', default=False),
     read_only_tmpfs=dict(type='bool'),
     recreate=dict(type='bool', default=False),
     restart_policy=dict(type='str'),
@@ -1150,6 +1150,11 @@
         before, after = sorted(list(set(before))), sorted(list(set(after)))
         return self._diff_update_and_compare('publish', before, after)
 
+    def diffparam_read_only(self):
+        before = self.info['hostconfig']['readonlyrootfs']
+        after = self.params['read_only']
+        return self._diff_update_and_compare('read_only', before, after)
+
     def diffparam_rm(self):
         before = self.info['hostconfig']['autoremove']
         after = self.params['rm']
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
containers.podman         1.4.1  
containers.podman             1.5.0  
containers.podman 1.9.1  
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  conmon:

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux

  security:
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I am really not sure what you are saying, but my guess is 
```
$ podman run --replace --name readonly --read-only=true fedora echo hi
642f2f870555343ef41e59beeb9368e24027e2db71dea42442991ab4db49f3f2
hi
$ podman run --replace --name readonly --read-only=false fedora touch /dan
42a48d4252260f57642462ce7805ad995bf8e62426e736b8e17e3884d5babd7e
```
This seems to work fine.
oh god. sorry. I sent this to the wrong (but very related!) app. it's meant to be for the ansible podman container thingo. Sigh. :( I'll just copy it over and close this.
